message:
refactor(indexer+lib): drop .index from LexicalRebuildFinalMergeArtifact (report .docs + .segments directly) and route doctor index probe through searchable_index_summary

Follow-up to commits bd493536 (federated search reader) and 1b48d17e
(searchable_index_* sweep). The staged lexical rebuild's final
artifact was still hanging onto a full `Index` just so callers (and
tests) could open a reader and count docs / segments. That coupled
the artifact lifetime to a live Tantivy Index handle and forced every
caller to pay the cost of opening a reader even when they only
needed two numbers. And the doctor probe was still calling
`frankensearch::lexical::cass_open_search_reader` directly, missing
the federated path.

src/indexer/mod.rs
- `LexicalRebuildFinalMergeArtifact` now reports `{ publish_path,
  docs: usize, segments: usize }` instead of
  `{ publish_path, index: Index }`. The finalizer computes doc
  and segment counts at publish time and stores them on the artifact
  directly, so callers never need to open a reader on the way out.
- Test `finalize_staged_lexical_rebuild_publish_artifact_assembles_
  multiple_inputs_without_doc_remerge` is renamed to
  `finalize_staged_lexical_rebuild_publish_artifact_publishes_
  federated_multi_input_frontier_without_doc_remerge` to describe
  what actually happens now: multi-input finalization publishes the
  federated shard frontier (not a merged-doc index). Assertion
  bodies update accordingly:
  * `reader.searcher().num_docs()` → `final_artifact.docs`
  * `index.segment_count()` → `final_artifact.segments`
  * Comment text updated from "assemble a multi-segment publish
    generation instead of remerging docs" to "preserve the final
    shard frontier without remerging docs".
- Single-input finalization test (the non-renamed one above) also
  switches to `final_artifact.docs` instead of opening a reader.

src/lib.rs (`run_doctor`)
- `if index_meta_path.exists()` → `if
  searchable_index_exists(&index_path)` so doctor works on
  federated installs (same pattern as commit 1b48d17e applied
  everywhere else).
- Body of the branch: replace `cass_open_search_reader(...)` +
  `searcher.num_docs()` with `searchable_index_summary(&index_path)`
  which already aggregates doc counts across shards:
  * `Ok(Some(summary))` arm: `num_docs = summary.docs`, then runs
    the existing `add_check!("index", "pass", ...)` path.
  * New `Ok(None)` arm: when the index dir exists but no summary
    can be derived (manifest missing, corrupted, etc.), emit
    `add_check!("index", "fail", "Search index metadata is
    missing", true)` and flag `needs_rebuild = true`. Previously
    this case would have fallen through silently.
  * `Err(e)` arm: unchanged — still emits
    `"Cannot open index: {}"` and `needs_rebuild = true`.

No callers outside these two files held the old `.index` field, so
this is a contained surface change. Removes one Tantivy-reader
lifetime dependency per publish-and-commit cycle.
